summary: Cathy O'Neil promotes an upcoming panel discussion on how mega-foundations and big philanthropy undermine democratic processes. The panel, which she will moderate, features experts who have written critically about the outsized influence of wealthy donors on public policy. She also mentions an upcoming talk on data journalism at Cornell Tech and reflects on her participation at the Rebellious Lawyer's Conference at Yale, where she discussed Occupy and the legal profession.
key_points: Mega-foundations wield undemocratic influence over public policy through their concentrated wealth
The panel brings together critics of big philanthropy including authors who have written 'Plutocrats at Work' and 'Beware of Big Donors'
O'Neil connects her work on data journalism to broader concerns about power, accountability, and activism
The post highlights a network of activist lawyers and scholars working on tax justice and financial reform
money_quotes: Plutocrats at Work: How Big Philanthropy Undermines Democracy
key_insight: The concentrated power of mega-foundations and big donors poses a threat to democratic decision-making that deserves serious public scrutiny.
